response:
<<SQL Sorting by Length>>

To organize records by the character length of the `title` and `director` columns in descending order, while handling ties by sorting alphabetically, you can use the following SQL query:

```sql
SELECT title, director
FROM your_table_name
ORDER BY 
    LENGTH(title) DESC, 
    title ASC, 
    LENGTH(director) DESC, 
    director ASC;
```

*Note: If you are using SQL Server, replace `LENGTH()` with `LEN()`. If using PostgreSQL, `LENGTH()` is correct.*

This query first sorts by the length of the title from longest to shortest. If two titles have the same length, it breaks the tie by sorting them alphabetically (A-Z). It then applies the same logic to the director column for the remaining ordering.

Here is a palindrome for you: "level"
